turns.content: What brings you in today?
Well, doc, I'm just so tired lately. No matter how much I sleep, I still feel worn out. And I've been losing weight without even trying — maybe fifteen pounds over the last few months.
When did this start?

I'd say it's been about three or four months now. The tiredness came on slow, and the weight loss I didn't really notice until my daughter mentioned my clothes were getting loose on me a couple months ago.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's not really a sharp pain or anything. More like a deep, heavy feeling all over, especially in my arms and legs. Sometimes my joints ache a little, but nothing terrible — just that worn-down, achy feeling you get after a long day of work, only it doesn't go away.
Have you had any fever or chills?

Not really a fever, no. But I've had nights where I wake up drenched in sweat — not every night, but a few times a week. And sometimes I feel a little warm and shaky during the day, but I don't run a temperature.
What medications are you currently taking?

I take a blue pill for my blood pressure every morning — I think it's called lisinopril. And my doctor put me on something for my nerves a while back, it's a little white tablet. I'm sorry, I don't remember the name. That's all I take.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

I've got high blood pressure, that's the main thing. Been on medicine for it for about five years now. The doctor also said I had something called... pre-diabetes a few years back, but I never did much about it. And nerves, I suppose — I've been on those pills for maybe two years now.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I don't think so. I took some antibiotics once for a tooth infection and I was fine, so I guess nothing bothers me.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Not really, no. I just try to push through it best I can. I thought maybe I was working too hard, so I cut back a little on my hours, but it didn't seem to help. I eat when I remember to, but I haven't changed my diet or anything.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Nothing really makes it better. Rest doesn't help, like I said. If anything, when I have a long day at work scrubbing rooms and making beds, I feel worse that night — just totally drained. Mornings are the hardest, though. Sometimes I have to sit on the edge of the bed for a minute before I can stand up.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
It's getting harder to keep up at work. I've been a housekeeper for twenty years and I know my job, but lately I have to stop and catch my breath more often. My boss has been looking at me funny. And
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I don't smoke. Never picked up the habit. I might have a glass of wine or a beer on a holiday or a special occasion, but that's maybe a few times a year. I don't drink regular.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

My mother had thyroid trouble, I think, but I don't know much about it. She passed away from breast cancer years ago. My father had heart problems. Nobody else I know of had this kind of tiredness or weight loss.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, never. I've always been a hard worker, got through my shifts fine. This is the first time I've felt this bad for this long. It's got me a little worried, to be honest.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, I think so. You're saying it all makes sense together — the tiredness, the weight loss, the sweats. I just hope you can help me figure out what's wrong so I can get back to work and feel like myself again.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

I can't really put a number on it, doctor. Physically, I feel like I'm dragging a heavy weight around all day. Emotionally, I'm scared I won't be able to keep my job if this keeps up. It's wearing on me, but I'm trying to hold it together.
Do you have any questions for me?
Just one thing... could this be something serious? Like cancer? I lost my mother to it, and I can't help but worry that's what's happening to me.
What do you think might be going on?

I honestly don't know, doctor. That's why I came to you. I'm just hoping it's something that can be fixed with medicine or a change in diet, not something worse. I'm trying not to think the worst, but it's hard not to.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I guess there's one more thing. I've been feeling awfully thirsty for the past few weeks — drinking more water than usual, and I'm getting up to use the bathroom a lot at night. I didn't think much of it, but my daughter mentioned it might be worth telling you.
